case_title: JUDITH LEASH VS. JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION

docket_entries.description: SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF SEP-15-2025 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF SEP-15-2025 BY VISITING JUDGE CINDEE MAYFIELD. PLAINTIFF JUDITH LEASH APPEARED. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION APPEARED, REPRESENTED BY LEA LITONJUA, VICE PRESIDENT - BRANCH MANAGER. PROOF OF SERVICE ON FILE. BOTH EXHIBITS RETAINED. JUDGE CINDEE MAYFIELD, CLERK: A. MOK. NOT REPORTED. (506)
DECLARATION OF APPEARANCE ON BEHALF OF CORPORATION PURSUANT TO CCP 116.540 (B)-(H) LEA LITONJUA FILED BY DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION
EXHIBITS FROM EXHIBIT REPORT DATED DEC-01-2025 ORDERED DESTROYED
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF SERVED AUG-10-2025, COURT CERTIFIED MAIL RETURNED SERVED AS TO DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION
SERVICE OF COMPLAINT BY CERTIFIED MAIL FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEASH, JUDITH AS TO DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF LEASH, JUDITH IS NOT OWED ANY MONEY BY DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION CHASE REQUIRES ITS CUSTOMERS TO REPORT SUSPICIOUS, POTENTIALLY ALTERED CHECKS WITHIN 60 DAYS OF THE CHECK BEING CASHED. CHASE OFFERS A FREE SERVICE WHICH SHOWS CUSTOMERS THE FRONT AND BACK OF CANCELLED CHECKS TO ASSIST IN PROMPTLY IDENTIFYING ALTERED CHECKS. HERE, PLAINTIFF DID NOT NOTIFY CHASE THAT CHECK 102 HAD BEEN ALTERED UNTIL 9-10 MONTHS AFTER THE CHECK WAS CASHED, THUS SHE DID NOT CARRY HER BURDEN OF PROOF. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS, SEE JUDGMENT ATTACHED PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF SEP-15-2025 - JUDGMENT.
MONEY CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $7,120.00 FILED BY LEASH, JUDITH AS TO DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR SEP-15-2025 AT 03:30 PM IN DEPT 506
